panels.title: Build
panels.description: host-agent image version, detected chassis, and loaded profile for each host. Single info metric host_agent_info{version,chassis,profile} written by the vmagent run script at container start, surfaced via node-exporter's textfile collector.

panels.title: CPU
panels.description: Hottest core per socket via coretemp (Linux universal). Falls back through IPMI on hosts whose BMC exposes per-CPU sensors. CPU 2 is empty on single-socket hosts; that's expected.

panels.title: Drives
panels.description: Current SMART temp per physical drive, tagged HDD or SSD via smartctl_device_rotation_rate. Label priority: Unraid slot (disk1/parity/cache) via unraid_disk_info join, then MegaRAID slot N stripped from bus_0_megaraid_N, otherwise the bare Linux device (sda, sdb). HDD thresholds yellow≥45C red≥55C; SSD yellow≥55C red≥65C.

targets.expr:
sort_by_label(
  label_replace(smartctl_device_temperature{host=~"$host",temperature_type="current"} and on(host,device) smartctl_device_rotation_rate, "slot", "$1", "device", "(?:bus_0_(?:sat\\+)?megaraid_)?(.+)") unless on(host,device) unraid_disk_info
  or
  (smartctl_device_temperature{host=~"$host",temperature_type="current"} and on(host,device) smartctl_device_rotation_rate) * on(host,device) group_left(slot, sort_key) unraid_disk_info,
  "sort_key", "slot"
)

sort_by_label(
  label_replace(smartctl_device_temperature{host=~"$host",temperature_type="current"} unless on(host,device) smartctl_device_rotation_rate, "slot", "$1", "device", "(?:bus_0_(?:sat\\+)?megaraid_)?(.+)") unless on(host,device) unraid_disk_info
  or
  (smartctl_device_temperature{host=~"$host",temperature_type="current"} unless on(host,device) smartctl_device_rotation_rate) * on(host,device) group_left(slot, sort_key) unraid_disk_info,
  "sort_key", "slot"
)

panels.title: Fans
panels.description: Controller setpoint (% the fan-controller is commanding chassis fans to spin at) + actual measured fan speeds. Chassis fans show RPM via IPMI. GPU fans show % (via nvidia-smi), labeled by GPU model name (A5500, P4, etc.) so you can tell which card is spinning. Read together with the Temperatures panel — setpoint is what the controller decided based on those temps; the GPU fan is the card's OWN fan, which drives chassis assist when it saturates (v0.1.3+).

panels.title: Temperatures
panels.description: IPMI temperature sensors + per-drive SMART temps over time. The Fans strip above shows what the controller did with these temps.

panels.title: GPU
panels.description: GPU die temperature — lives in the temperatures section because it's a thermal signal that feeds the fan controller, not a usage metric.

panels.title: Open FDs
panels.description: Approaching max = can't add more containers

panels.title: Load Average vs Cores
panels.description: Load above the red line = bottleneck

panels.title: Memory Breakdown
panels.description: Used = app memory. If Used fills the chart, no room for more containers

panels.title: Latency
panels.description: sda avg ms per op (>10ms = slow)

panels.title: Latency
panels.description: sdb avg ms per op (>10ms = slow)

panels.title: Latency
panels.description: sdc avg ms per op (>10ms = slow)

panels.title: Latency
panels.description: sdd avg ms per op (>10ms = slow)
